title: Streamee Website
clientType: Streamee: radio, music, and podcast platform
summary: I redesigned Streamee's web app in Figma around the moment you press play: browse a station, find a show, save something for later, or spend coins on a contest.
problem: Streamee brings radio, podcasts, moods, and contests into the same app. The interface needed to feel like a place to listen, not a pile of separate destinations.

cover.alt: Streamee home screen with sidebar navigation, a Greek Hits hero, content shelves, and a persistent audio player

sections.heading: Context
sections.body: Streamee sits between a radio directory and an on-demand player. The live site carries radio stations and podcasts, while moods and contests add more ways to decide what to play or how to spend time.

sections.heading: Challenge
sections.body: The hard part was keeping the catalogue from taking over. Home needs to get you into a stream quickly. Podcasts needs to support browsing and episode detail. Contests needs to explain the prize and the coin balance. They share the same chrome, but they cannot all behave like the same page.

sections.heading: Process
sections.body: I kept the player pinned to the bottom and put the main routes in a left rail. The Home screen starts with a featured mood, then moves into rows of favourites and Originals. Search and collection pages use large hit areas and short search chips. Detail screens give the artwork room, then put play, follow, save, and share beside the thing you are looking at.

sections.heading: Output
sections.body: The Figma file grew into a full set of product screens: home, radio discovery, station detail, podcasts, podcast detail, moods, search, library empty and populated states, accounts, settings, and contests. I chose the screens here because they show how the same player and navigation carry across the app.

sections.heading: Result
sections.body: The redesign makes Streamee read as one listening product. Move from a mood to a show, from a station to something saved, or from the home feed to a contest without leaving the same frame. The player stays put. The page does the explaining.
